frame_analyses.description: Laparo Aspire trainer setup view, no instruments visible yet
Interior view of trainer showing circular marking on gauze suspended between clamps
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Gauze properly positioned with 4cm diameter circle marked, secured by clamps on both sides

frame_analyses.description: Close-up view of gauze with circular cutting pattern clearly visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pattern appears well-defined with clear 4cm diameter marking

frame_analyses.description: Setup phase continuing, no cutting activity yet observed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extended setup time suggests preparation or hesitation

frame_analyses.description: Black instrument (appears to be grasper) visible in field approaching gauze
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First instrument appearance, beginning of timed portion

frame_analyses.description: Instrument appears to be making initial contact with gauze at marked line
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting appears to begin at appropriate location on circumference

frame_analyses.description: Continued cutting motion visible, following the circular pattern
Final exterior view of trainer, task appears complete

confidence_rationale: Video quality and angles make it impossible to accurately assess cutting precision or measure deviations from the marked line. Task completion and gauze integrity can be observed, but critical scoring elements are not visible.
cannot_determine: Exact deviation measurements at 8 cardinal points due to video angle and resolution
Precise cutting accuracy - camera angle does not allow measurement of deviation from marked line
Whether cutting stayed within 2mm tolerance throughout the circumference
Actual start time of cutting activity - instruments not clearly visible until late in video
technique_summary: Task appears completed within time limit but with extended duration suggesting novice-level performance. Critical assessment of cutting precision cannot be made from available video footage.
strengths: Task completed within maximum time limit
Gauze remained secured to clamps throughout procedure
improvement_suggestions: Reduce overall completion time from 270s toward intermediate range (90-180s)
Cannot assess cutting precision from this video quality
